title: zehneria cunninghamii
intro: Zehneria cunninghamii, also known as the Australian Creeper, is a type of flowering plant native to eastern Australia. It is a perennial climber with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. In traditional herbal medicine, Zehneria cunninghamii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used topically in the form of a poultice or salve.
